description: Secured freight for passenger and all-cargo aircraft
meaning_for_cortex: Cargo may be eligible for passenger aircraft from a security standpoint if other blocks pass.
notes: Do not confuse security eligibility with DG/legal/carrier eligibility.

description: Cargo secure for all-cargo aircraft only
meaning_for_cortex: Security status allows all-cargo aircraft only; do not route on passenger belly unless status changes to passenger-eligible.
notes: Important for deciding passenger belly vs freighter/security routing.

description: Cargo not secured yet for passenger or all-cargo aircraft
meaning_for_cortex: Cargo is not security-ready. It requires screening/security process before loading.
notes: Hard gate until security status is resolved.

description: Security cargo - personal effects requiring prescreening
meaning_for_cortex: Personal effects or similar cargo need prescreening/security handling before uplift.

description: Cargo secure for passenger and all-cargo aircraft according to high-risk requirements
meaning_for_cortex: High-risk cargo that has been secured under enhanced requirements. Still needs route/station validation.
notes: Never treat SHR as ordinary cargo. Keep security confidence capped without proof.

meaning_for_cortex: Theft-sensitive cargo requiring secure storage, chain of custody, controlled handover and often escort.
notes: Security status and secure storage are both needed; Block C/D/G interaction.

meaning_for_cortex: Cargo has theft, tamper, damage or vulnerability risk and needs controlled handling.
notes: Treat like security-sensitive even if not high-value.

meaning_for_cortex: Reserved/controlled booking cargo; security and operational coordination should be confirmed.
notes: Use mostly as booking/security coordination warning.

meaning_for_cortex: Fast transfer cargo; security status must be clear before transfer because there is little time to resolve screening gaps.
notes: If security status unknown, QRT is risky.
